title: dryas octopetala subsp. integrifolia
intro: Dryas octopetala subsp. integrifolia, also known as the mountain avens, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of mountain avens include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Mountain avens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin issues and respiratory problems.
